name: Slippery Elm
summary: Slippery elm is a mucilage-containing bark used traditionally for GI soothing, but clinical evidence remains insufficient.
description: Slippery elm bark provides mucilage that forms a protective coating, which can reduce local irritation and support comfort in the GI tract.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v10.4: Traditional demulcent use is not enough for monetized evidence-backed recommendation. | Source citation missing in workbook; queued for PMID/source verification before publication.
claimMap.notes: v10.4: Traditional demulcent use is not enough for monetized evidence-backed recommendation. | Source citation missing in workbook; queued for PMID/source verification before publication.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: No well-supported universal clinical dose; product-specific demulcent use

sources.title: v9.4: Mostly animal/preclinical and influencer hype; keep out of monetization until human evidence exists. | Source citation missing in workbook; queued for PMID/source verification before publication.
